text.title: A/B Testing Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding A/B Testing Basics. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Before traffic is split, a pre-registration MUST exist with: hypothesis, primary metric, ≤2 secondary metrics, MDE, computed sample size, pre-registered close date. No back-fill.

rationale.source: Ron Kohavi, Trustworthy Online Controlled Experiments (2020)

rule.rationale: Pre-registration prevents HARKing and locks the analysis plan.

rule.statement: Default split MUST be 50/50. Non-50/50 splits require numeric justification + explicit power adjustment in the pre-reg.
rule.rationale: 50/50 maximizes statistical power for given sample.

rule.statement: No decision MUST be made before the pre-registered close date / sample size, whichever later. Early stops require pre-registered alpha-spending procedure (e.g. Bayesian or O'Brien-Fleming).
rule.rationale: Peeking inflates false-positive rate to 30%+ with naïve continuation.

rule.statement: On close, sample-ratio mismatch (SRM) check MUST be run. SRM with chi-square p<0.001 OR observed split deviation >5% invalidates the test.
rule.rationale: SRM signals broken assignment; downstream conclusions are unsound.

rule.statement: Secondary metrics (≤2) MUST be declared in the pre-reg. Post-hoc additions are forbidden; any new metric requires a new test.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc additions multiply false discovery rate.
